headline: 3 States, 3 Land Policies, One Constitutional Question
context: Johor, Penang, and Selangor each announced different frameworks for converting Malay Reserve Land. All cite the same court ruling.

cards.big: Three states. One court ruling. Three interpretations.
cards.sub: Johor: 70-30 allocation. Penang: market-rate. Selangor: trust model.

cards.big: 70% of flagged land is owned by GLCs, not individuals.
cards.sub: The 'protecting Malay landowners' narrative mainly protects corporations.

cards.big: Orang Asli face the same pressure. Zero coverage.
cards.sub: Framing as Malay-Chinese erases indigenous communities.

cards.big: Singapore solved this without ethnic allocation.
cards.sub: GLS system: 28% affordable housing in mixed developments.

cards.big: Who actually benefits from the current restrictions?
cards.sub: Follow the title deeds, not the headlines.

cards.big: The debate uses ethnic lenses while the landowners are corporations. The question is wrong before the answer begins.
